Courses in 2025-2026 Academic Catalog - DRAFT COPY 597

ECON709 Advanced Topics in Applied and Theoretical Macroeconomics (2 Credits)

Read, discuss, and analyze current topics in macroeconomics, including asset pricing models, models of economic growth, investment, and

the labor market. Specific topics covered will change from semester to semester depending on the students’ and faculty's interests. Intended primarily for students beginning thesis research in economics. Prerequisite: Must have completed a one-year graduate sequence in one of the macroeconomic fields.

Repeatable to: 12 credits if content differs.
